mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels across all tested periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, triggering short-term equity sell-offs within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate output and positive equity returns over the next month during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero or negative predictive power for 20-day SPX returns during mid-cycle expansion periods, or out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a stable foundation for wage growth and consumer expenditure, supporting sustained corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth exhibits no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across multiple macro regimes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer prices triggers immediate market expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.3%, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Convergence of strong real GDP growth and rising retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or interaction effects show negative predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained slowdown in industrial production growth below 0.5% monthly signals supply-side stagnation, which reduces corporate output expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO MoM growth falls below 0.5% during late-cycle phases, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.
